# Validation

This document can be used to validate the inputs and result of the execution of the upgrade transaction which you are
signing.

The steps are:

1. [Validate the Domain and Message Hashes](#expected-domain-and-message-hashes)
2. [Transaction Inputs](config.toml): inputs can be verified in the config.toml file, which includes a link to the source betanet `chain.yaml`.
3. State Changes: the template's `_validate` block includes assertions to confirm the task ran correctly. State Changes can also be manually reviewed in Tenderly, using the link shown in the terminal during simulation.

## Expected Domain and Message Hashes

First, we need to validate the domain and message hashes. These values should match both the values on your ledger and
the values printed to the terminal when you run the task.

> [!CAUTION]
>
> Before signing, ensure the below hashes match what is on your ledger.
>
> ### Betanet 1/1 Safe (`0x8E851F7d8bAeaD95F592847a020cAC7A062dafd9`)
>
> - Domain Hash: `0x85cc686e8cbc7571a70994af7e216c5525d22203d359558d46a795125c38de14`
> - Message Hash: `0xe6441aa8f4e1e463f393f808e4b6a86ee84f6e19cf44dc00072597debf1f1da6`

> [!NOTE]
> These hashes were produced with the ProxyAdminOwner Safe nonce pinned to `7` in
> [config.toml](config.toml) `[stateOverrides]`. If the on-chain nonce has advanced, update the
> override and re-run `just --dotenv-path $(pwd)/.env simulate` — the message hash (and the
> `Data to sign`) will change and must be refreshed here.

## State Changes

Per chain, exactly one `DisputeGameFactoryProxy` slot is rewritten — the `CANNON_KONA` (game
type 8) implementation slot — re-pointing it at the same impl (`0x2DDA3584b51eF5236f7726Dea5A0FB6B3cA94AeC`)
with `gameArgs` carrying the new prestate `0x0335abee…307a28`. The `setInitBond` call is a no-op
(bond unchanged at `0.08 ETH`). No other contracts are touched.

- karst-u19-beta-0 DisputeGameFactory: `0x313bcDa9f2D76Bab3554436a434A41779dAC74d0`
- karst-u19-beta-1 DisputeGameFactory: `0x86E719dB26f3684C529D18B773939BC0Fc0fC94d`

